topics.banner: Topics allow you to organize your monitoring strategy by grouping related interest areas into distinct categories. This logical structure helps you track visibility across different segments of your brand or industry. For example, a travel agency might create separate topics for 'Summer Cruises', 'Winter Ski Trips', and 'Luxury Safaris'. By categorizing your prompts, you can better analyze how AI platforms perceive different aspects of your business and identify which specific product lines need improved visibility.

prompts.title: Prompts
prompts.noPrompts: No prompts in this topic yet. Add prompts to start monitoring.
prompts.textPlaceholder: e.g. Boats for rental in Brasil
prompts.charLimitExceeded: Prompt text must not exceed {{max}} characters
prompts.banner: Prompts are the specific questions or instructions you send to AI models to test what they say about your brand. Well-crafted prompts simulate real customer queries, allowing you to monitor mention frequency and response quality across multiple platforms simultaneously. For example, instead of just 'Boat rental', try 'What are the best boat rental services in Brazil?' or 'Compare luxury yacht charters for 2026'. Use this section to manage your library of test cases and track how AI answers evolve over time for each specific inquiry.

hero.title: AI is talking
hero.subtitle: 230 million people asked ChatGPT a question last week. Many of them were asking about your industry. If your brand doesn't show up in those answers — someone else's does.

multiModel.description: We ask ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok, and Perplexity the exact same question about your industry. You see which ones recommend you — and which ones are sending your customers to competitors.

webSearch.title: Some AIs pretend to find you
webSearch.description: Certain AI models claim they search the web — but never surface your content. We test which platforms actually pull real, up-to-date data and which ones quietly leave you out.

topicOrg.title: Track every question that matters
topicOrg.description: Monitor how AI answers questions about your brand, products, services, and competitors. Organize by topic, track changes week by week, and spot problems before they cost you customers.

analytics.title: Your AI visibility score, updated weekly
analytics.description: Mention frequency, response quality, competitor comparisons, and anomaly alerts — all in one scorecard. The moment an AI stops recommending you, you'll know.

i18n.title: Different language, different visibility
i18n.description: Ask the same question in English, Spanish, and Portuguese — you'll get completely different answers. Are you visible in all the markets you serve, or invisible in some?

timeline.title: Monitoring Timeline

sources.title: Sources
sources.description: Explore AI answer sources for your organization.
sources.name: Name
sources.banner: Sources represent the foundations of knowledge that AI models use to ground their answers about your organization. This section tracks the domains, URLs, and specific text annotations that AI scrapers reference when generating responses. For example, you can see if an AI is pulling data from your official documentation, a news article, or a competitor's blog. Understanding these sources allows you to identify which parts of your online presence are effectively feeding the AI 'brain' and where your brand’s information might be missing or misinterpreted.

llmText.verifyError: An error occurred while verifying llms.txt
llmText.deployInstruction1: To make this file available to AI scrapers and search platforms, you must host it at the root of your main domain.
llmText.deployStep1: Download the generated llms.txt file using the download button.
llmText.deployStep2: Upload the text file to the "public" folder (or root directory) of your hosting provider.
llmText.deployStep3: Ensure it is publicly accessible without authentication.
llmText.deployStep4: Click "Verify Live llms.txt" to check if our servers can detect the correct version.
llmText.suggestionsSubtitle: Based on your extracted website content.

models.title: Configuring AI Models & Platforms
models.p1: This is where you control which AI platforms Ainalytics monitors for your brand. You can toggle support for ChatGPT (OpenAI), Gemini (Google), Claude (Anthropic), and other major LLMs.
models.p2: For each platform, you can select specific models and decide whether to enable web search. Web-enabled models simulate how users interact with live AI specialized in current events, while standard models show baseline training data perception.
models.p3: We recommend activating at least one model per major platform to get a representative view of the AI ecosystem. Your billing is typically based on the number of active models and the frequency of prompt executions.

topics.title: Organizing Your Strategy
topics.p1: Topics are the pillars of your monitoring strategy. They allow you to group related prompts into distinct business areas, such as specific product lines, market segments, or competitor groups.
topics.p2: By categorizing your visibility monitoring, you can identify which areas of your business are well-represented in AI answers and which ones require more content optimization. For example, you might have one topic for 'Service Excellence' and another for 'Technical Documentation'.
topics.p3: We recommend starting with 3-5 core topics that represent your primary value propositions. As you grow, you can add more specialized segments to get a granular view of your global AI footprint.

prompts.title: Mastering AI Visibility with Prompts
prompts.p1: Prompts are the exact questions customers ask AI tools when looking for services like yours. Creating effective prompts is key to understanding whether AI platforms are recommending you accurately or sending traffic to your competition.
prompts.p2: Instead of using generic keywords, try phrasing prompts as natural questions: 'What is the best boat rental in Brazil?' or 'Compare luxury yacht charters for 2026'. This simulates real-world usage and provides the most actionable data.
prompts.p3: Ainalytics automatically runs these prompts across multiple platforms in the background. You don't need to manually check each one; our system gathers the answers and analyzes mention frequency and sentiment for you.

sources.title: Uncovering the Foundations of AI Knowledge
sources.p1: Ever wondered where ChatGPT gets its information about you? The Sources page reveals the specific websites, documents, and news articles that AI platforms use to ground their answers.
sources.p2: By tracking these citations, you can see if AI is pulling from your official site or from third-party reviews. If you find that a competitor's blog is being cited instead of your own documentation, you know exactly where you need to strengthen your content strategy.
sources.p3: Click on any source to see which of your prompts are referencing it. This direct link between prompts and sources helps you understand the 'why' behind AI-generated responses.

llmText.title: Becoming AI-Readable with LLMs.txt
llmText.p1: AI scrapers 'read' the web differently than humans. The LLMs.txt tool helps you generate a specialized file that provides a clear, concise summary of your business in a format optimized for Large Language Models.
llmText.p2: By hosting an llms.txt file on your domain, you facilitate the work of AI scrapers, ensuring they capture the most accurate and up-to-date information about your brand, rather than relying on outdated or incomplete third-party data.
llmText.p3: Simply enter your domain, and we'll extract the key information, verify your sitemap, and even suggest new topics and prompts based on what we find on your site. It's the ultimate 'SEO for AI' tool.

insights.title: AI-Powered Strategy Insights
insights.p1: Beyond just showing you the data, Ainalytics uses advanced logic to generate actionable insights. We analyze trends across all your prompts and models to identify emerging risks or new opportunities for visibility.
insights.p2: These insights are generated periodically as our system accumulates enough data points to ensure statistical significance. If this page is empty, don't worry—it means our engines are still 'learning' your brand's unique visibility patterns.
insights.p3: Check back weekly to see recommendations on how to adjust your content or prompts to maximize your share-of-voice in the AI landscape.

welcome.title: Welcome to Ainalytics
welcome.description: Ainalytics monitors what AI platforms say about your brand across ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Grok and more. Let's walk you through the core features of the platform.
welcome.detail: In the next steps, you'll learn how to analyze your company, create topics & prompts, select AI models, and track your visibility results.

0.title: Topics & Categories
0.desc: Group your prompts logically into distinct categories for better organization and monitoring.

1.title: Prompts
1.desc: Manage your AI inputs and instructions to test against multiple platforms and models.

2.title: Sources
2.desc: Organize reference materials and knowledge bases that ground your AI responses.

3.title: LLMs.txt Generation
3.desc: Automatically generate an llms.txt file based on your website, ready for external LLMs to consume.

company.title: Analyze Your Company
company.description: Our GEO analyzer crawls your website, checks technical factors like robots.txt, sitemap, and structured data, then generates an AI-powered report with actionable insights.
company.detail: Understand exactly how AI platforms see your website — from content quality to technical readiness. This is your starting point for GEO optimization.

0.title: Website Crawling
0.desc: We scan your pages, meta tags, schema markup, and all technical assets.

1.title: 25 GEO Factors
1.desc: Each page is scored across 25 optimization factors in 4 categories.

2.title: AI Report
2.desc: Get a detailed AI analysis with strengths, weaknesses, and recommendations.

models.title: Select AI Models
models.description: Choose which AI platforms to monitor — ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok, and more. Each platform sees your brand differently, and you need visibility across all of them.
models.detail: Different AI models have different training data and behaviors. By monitoring multiple platforms, you get a complete picture of your AI visibility landscape.

0.title: Multi-Platform
0.desc: Monitor 5+ major AI platforms simultaneously in one dashboard.

1.title: Model Selection
1.desc: Choose specific models per platform for granular visibility tracking.

2.title: Web Search Mode
2.desc: Test how AI responds with and without real-time web search enabled.

topics.title: Select Your Topics
topics.description: Topics are categories that represent your products, services, or market segments. Select the ones relevant to your business to organize your monitoring.
topics.detail: Choose between 1 and 5 topics. You can always add more later from your dashboard.

brand.title: Brand Reputation
brand.desc: Monitor how AI platforms perceive and recommend your brand overall.

products.title: Products & Services
products.desc: Track mentions and recommendations of your specific offerings.

industry.title: Industry Trends
industry.desc: See how AI platforms discuss your industry and where you fit in.

compete.title: Competitive Landscape
compete.desc: Compare your visibility against competitors in AI recommendations.

prompts.title: Choose Your Prompts
prompts.description: Prompts are the real questions customers ask AI platforms. Select prompts for each topic to simulate searches and see if you're being recommended.
prompts.detail: Each topic should have at least 1 prompt. Prompts run automatically across all your selected platforms.

0.title: What is [Company]?
0.desc: Tests basic brand recognition and how AI describes your company.

1.title: Is [Company] trustworthy?
1.desc: Measures reputation and trust signals associated with your brand.

2.title: Reviews of [Company]
2.desc: Checks what AI surfaces about customer reviews and satisfaction.

0.title: Best [product type] providers
0.desc: Tests if AI recommends your products in category searches.

1.title: [Product] vs alternatives
1.desc: Compares how AI positions your offering against competitors.

2.title: How to choose a [product type]?
2.desc: Checks if your product appears in buying-guide responses.

0.title: Top companies in [industry]
0.desc: Tests if AI includes you among industry leaders.

1.title: Trends in [industry] 2025
1.desc: Checks if your brand appears in forward-looking industry discussions.

2.title: [Industry] market leaders
2.desc: Measures your positioning in market-leadership queries.

0.title: [Company] vs [Competitor]
0.desc: Direct comparison queries between you and key competitors.

1.title: Best alternative to [Competitor]
1.desc: Tests if AI suggests you as an alternative to competitors.

2.title: Why choose [Company] over others?
2.desc: Evaluates AI's reasoning for recommending your brand.

results.title: Analyze Results
results.description: Your dashboard shows visibility scores, mention frequency, source citations, and competitive comparisons — all in one place. See exactly where you stand in the AI landscape.
results.detail: From high-level scorecards to individual answer analysis, every data point helps you understand and improve your AI visibility.

0.title: Visibility Scores
0.desc: Track your mention rate and sentiment across all monitored AI platforms.

1.title: Source Analysis
1.desc: See which websites AI cites when talking about you or your competitors.

2.title: Trend Insights
2.desc: Weekly insights show emerging risks and opportunities in your AI visibility.

analyze.title: See Your GEO Score
analyze.subtitle: Get a free preview of your website's AI readiness
analyze.description: Enter your domain and we'll perform a quick homepage analysis — checking robots.txt, sitemap, llms.txt, and 25 GEO optimization factors to give you an instant AI readiness score.
analyze.wait: Crawling your homepage and computing GEO factors — this takes about 10 seconds...
analyze.unlockFull: Subscribe to see the full report
analyze.improvementsFound: metrics analyzed and improvement opportunities identified
analyze.categoriesInfoSubtitle: Each category measures a different aspect of your AI readiness

categoryExplain.technical: Technical Infrastructure covers whether AI crawlers can physically reach and parse your site. This includes SSR so the crawler sees actual HTML, robots.txt permissions, HTTPS for trust, page speed so it doesn't timeout, proper status codes, and sitemaps so it knows what pages exist. If these fail, nothing else matters because the AI never gets to your content. This is the largest category (~56% of total weight) because it's the prerequisite layer.
categoryExplain.content: Content Structure is about how your information is organized once the crawler gets to it. AI systems extract short chunks of 100–300 tokens and need each chunk to stand alone. This measures whether your headings create clean chunk boundaries, whether your first sentence directly answers the question (answer-first pattern), whether paragraphs are short and self-contained, and whether you use tables, lists, and FAQ sections with schema that AI can parse with high accuracy.
categoryExplain.authority: Authority & Trust measures whether AI systems believe your content enough to cite it. Freshness is one of the strongest citation signals — Perplexity weights it at roughly 40% of its ranking, and 76% of ChatGPT's most-cited pages were updated within 30 days. Other authority signals like original research quality and expert credentials require subjective judgment that can't be fully automated by crawling alone.
categoryExplain.semantic: Semantic/NLP measures whether AI correctly understands the meaning of your content at the language level. Readability scoring ensures the text matches patterns AI models were trained on — overly academic or jargon-heavy writing gets lower extraction confidence. Entity consistency checks whether you call the same thing by the same name across your entire site, because AI builds knowledge graphs and inconsistent naming creates confusion.
categoryExplain.summary: In short: Technical = can the AI get in, Content = can it extract cleanly, Authority = does it trust what it finds, Semantic = does it understand the meaning correctly. They form a natural funnel — each layer depends on the one before it.

0.q: How does AI visibility monitoring work?
0.a: We send your prompts to major AI platforms (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok) and analyze the responses to see if and how your brand is mentioned. Results are updated on a schedule based on your plan.

1.q: How often are my results updated?
1.a: Update frequency depends on your plan: Starter plans refresh monthly, Growth plans refresh weekly, and Business plans refresh daily. Custom plans can have custom refresh rates.

2.q: Can I change my plan at any time?
2.a: Yes! You can upgrade or change your plan at any time from the Plans page. When upgrading, you'll be charged a prorated amount for the remaining billing period.

3.a: LLMs.txt is a specialized file you host on your website that provides AI scrapers with accurate, structured information about your business. It helps ensure AI platforms have the most up-to-date data about your brand.

4.q: How do I improve my GEO score?
4.a: Your GEO score is based on 25 optimization factors across 4 categories: Technical, Content, Authority, and Semantic. Use the recommendations in your company analysis report to prioritize improvements.

5.q: Can I monitor multiple brands or clients?
5.a: Yes! With multi-tenant workspaces, agencies and consultants can manage separate monitoring for each client. Each brand gets its own workspace, prompts, and visibility reports.

full.aboutUsText:
AINalytics is a comprehensive AI visibility monitoring platform. We help businesses understand, measure, and improve how artificial intelligence systems perceive and represent their brand.

From ChatGPT and Claude to Gemini and Grok — we monitor every major AI platform in real time, giving you actionable insights to stay ahead of the competition in the AI-driven landscape.
full.problemText:
Today, millions of users rely on AI assistants to research companies, compare products, and make purchasing decisions. If AI platforms misrepresent your brand — or don't mention it at all — you're losing customers you'll never know about.

Traditional SEO doesn't address this. AI models form opinions based on structured data, authority signals, and content quality in ways that differ fundamentally from search engines.
solutionItems: Monitor AI responses about your brand across ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Grok in real time
Analyze how AI models perceive and position your brand versus competitors
Track citation sources to understand which content drives AI recommendations
Get GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) scores with actionable improvement plans
Generate llms.txt files to give AI systems accurate, structured brand information
Deep AI analysis powered by the most advanced models to uncover hidden opportunities
advantageDescs: The only platform monitoring your brand across all major AI systems simultaneously — not just one.
Stay ahead with continuous monitoring. Know instantly when AI responses about your brand change.
Our proprietary GEO scoring system analyzes 25+ factors to give you a clear optimization roadmap.
Understand exactly which web pages and data sources AI models use to form opinions about your brand.
Bank-level encryption, SOC 2 compliance, and strict tenant isolation protect your competitive intelligence.
Full support for English, Spanish, and Portuguese with locale-aware analysis and reporting.
